revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]][[it:Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]][[ja:イシコリドメ]][[ru:Исикори-домэ но микото]][[zh:伊斯許理度賣命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|metal casting|qid=Q9268371|1=Metal casting}}}}
{{short description|Shinto kami of mirrors}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''<ref>{{cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ishikoridome|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=75|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp|accessdate=29 May 2017|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=イシコリトメ【石凝姥命・(鋳し凝り臣)】・イシコリトメノマゴ【石凝姥の孫】|url=http://gejirin.com/src/I/isikoritome.html|website=gejirin.com|accessdate=29 May 2017}}</ref>|石凝姥命|extra=also {{Nihongo2|伊斯許理度売命}}<ref>{{cite web|title=イシコリドメ : 神社と古事記|url=http://www.buccyake-kojiki.com/archives/1003872379.html|website=神社と古事記|date=8 August 2004 |accessdate=29 May 2017|language=ja-JP}}</ref>}} is a [[kami]] of mirrors in [[Shinto]]. She was regarded as an ancestral deity of '''Kagami zukuri no muraji''' (The mirror-making clans). In [[Japanese mythology]], she created the exquisite ''[[Yata no Kagami|Yata-no-kagami]]'' mirror which lures the sun goddess [[Amaterasu]] out of her cave and returns light to the world.<ref>{{cite web|title=石凝姥 {{!}} イシコリドメ {{!}} 日本神話の世界|url=http://www.shinwanosekai.info/ishikoridome.php|website=www.shinwanosekai.info|accessdate=29 May 2017}}</ref> Due to this achievement, Ishikori-dome is worshipped by makers of mirrors and {{ill|stonemasonry|en|stonemasonry|qid=Q19794820|lt=stonecutters|1=Stonemasonry}}. She is worshiped as '''the god of casting and metalworking'''. She is enshrined in the Fuigo-jinja (Tennoji Ward, Osaka City), the Nakayama-jinja (Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture) the Kagamitsukurinimasu amaterumitama-jinja (Shiki-gun, Nara Prefecture), Iwayama-jinja (Niimi City, Okayama Prefecture). <ref>''Queer Myth, Symbol & Spirit'' by David Hatfield Sparks, Mariya Sparks, and Randy Luncunas Conner, published by Cassell in 1997</ref>
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/ishikoridome/ Ishikoridome] - History of Japan Database
